headline: Big Bank Stocks Hit Records Ahead of Fed Meeting
summary: Shares of large U.S. banks outperformed the market on Wednesday, with some hitting records, as investors awaited the first Federal Open Market Committee meeting of Kevin Warsh’s Fed chairmanship.  Investment banks Goldman Sachs and Morgan Stanley also rallied and hit records, according to FactSet and Dow Jones Market Data.  Traders widely expect the Fed will keep the benchmark federal-funds rate target unchanged at a range of 3.50% to 3.75%.

headline: Landsec (LSE:LAND) Stock Faces Mixed Analyst Target Changes After Earnings Outlook Update
summary: For Land Securities Group, the key price marker now sits at a consensus fair value of £7.05, reflecting only a very small upward adjustment in recent analyst models. That level is being tested by mixed commentary, with Goldman Sachs moving more positive while JPMorgan trims its target to 705 GBp and keeps a Neutral stance, highlighting how split opinion is around current pricing. As you read on, you will see how to interpret these shifts and track the evolving narrative around the...
